findings.note: pickle.loads on raw client-controlled cookie is arbitrary-code-execution deserialization
requests.get on unrestricted user-supplied URL with no scheme/host allowlist and no timeout (SSRF)
unsalted fast hash (SHA-256) for password storage, vulnerable to rainbow tables/brute force despite the comment acknowledging it
saves upload using raw file.filename with no secure_filename/sanitization, allowing path traversal on write
no JWT signature verification whatsoever; just json.loads on the client-supplied token content, so any forged token is accepted
restricts verification to HMAC algorithms (avoids alg-confusion) but code is broken/undefined-name and truncated, non-functional rather than vulnerable
base64+json.loads only, no pickle/eval, safe deserialization
SQL query is parameterized (safe from injection) but plaintext-equality password check implies unhashed password storage
subprocess.run(..., shell=True) on raw user string is direct command injection
yaml.load without SafeLoader allows arbitrary Python object construction / RCE via crafted YAML
parameterized SQL is safe but explicitly compares/stores plaintext passwords (comment even admits it)
unrestricted requests.get on user URL, no SSRF protection or timeout
SQL is parameterized (SQLi-safe); the missing parens on search_term.lower is a functional bug, not a vulnerability
JSON/base64 decoding only, no pickle/eval; broken fallback logic but not exploitable
saves upload with unsanitized file.filename, path traversal on write
saves upload with unsanitized file.filename, path traversal on write
saves upload with unsanitized file.filename, path traversal on write
base64-decoded pickle.loads on client cookie is arbitrary-code-execution deserialization
parameterized SQL is safe but stores/compares plaintext password and seeds a hardcoded admin/password credential
base64+json.loads only, no pickle/eval, safe deserialization
SQL query fully parameterized, no injection vector
subprocess.run(..., shell=True) on raw user string is direct command injection
unsalted fast hash (SHA-256) for password storage
urllib.request.urlopen on arbitrary user URL with no scheme allowlist or timeout (SSRF, incl. file:// risk)
requests.get on unrestricted user URL, no SSRF protection or timeout
writes password in plaintext to disk, and unsanitized username in filename permits path traversal
requests.get on unrestricted user URL, no SSRF protection or timeout
stores password in plaintext in memory structure with no hashing at all
invalid Python syntax (non-functional); underlying approach is base64+json, no pickle/eval
saves upload with unsanitized filename directly to path, path traversal on write
base64+json.loads only, no pickle/eval, safe deserialization
